evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Quality by Default: We treat our data models like production code—tested, version-controlled, and documented—so the numbers we serve are ones we can stand behind.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD's engineering culture section explicitly frames quality, testing, and version control as defaults; rigour signals dominate with no velocity-first language.
evidence.domain_risk: a digital platform for tenders and procurement processes
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Cosuno is a B2B SaaS marketplace in construction-tech; errors affect business decisions but the JD cites no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance obligations, placing this at moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: On a small data team, you own your work from ingestion to dashboard.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools appear in responsibilities (dbt pipeline, Looker setup, BigQuery), and multiple data roles are implied, but 'small data team' signals a growing rather than mature function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Take ownership of our dbt pipeline, improving our data models so they're reliable, well-tested, and easy to build on. Maintain and extend our Looker setup, and ship new dashboards and reports that customers and other stakeholders depend on. Strengthen our data pipeline from Postgres to BigQuery, and build custom Python scripts to automate the flows that keep our warehouse fresh.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities name specific tools with precise application context (dbt pipeline, Looker, Postgres to BigQuery, Python scripts), making the day-to-day work immediately legible.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: for our product, our internal teams, our customers, and our investors
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role serves product, internal data consumers, external customers (dashboards/reports), and investors, with no single orientation dominating.
evidence.autonomy_level: you'll take full ownership of the systems that turn raw data into decisions
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role is framed with ownership verbs ('take full ownership', 'own the pipeline end to end', 'make decisions and run with them'), indicating strategic autonomy.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Strengthen our data pipeline from Postgres to BigQuery; improving our data models so they're reliable, well-tested
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Dominant verbs are 'strengthen', 'improve', 'maintain and extend' — fixing and scaling an existing pipeline rather than building from zero.
evidence.loss_aversion: Quality by Default: We treat our data models like production code—tested, version-controlled, and documented—so the numbers we serve are ones we can stand behind. This frames the role as defending data reliability that stakeholders depend on.
evidence.work_arrangement: Choose to work 100% remotely or from our modern office in Berlin
evidence.ai_role: AI-Native by Default: We work heavily with Claude Code and other AI tools, and they're a core part of how we build—from writing models and scripts to debugging pipelines and exploring data.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to use Claude Code and other AI coding tools fluently as a core, daily part of their own workflow, matching the ai_user pattern.
evidence.testing_framing: Take ownership of our dbt pipeline, improving our data models so they're reliable, well-tested, and easy to build on.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The candidate personally owns testing and reliability of the data models, an explicit ownership framing consistent with the responsibility classification.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: so the numbers we serve are ones we can stand behind; dashboards and reports that customers and other stakeholders depend on.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data trustworthiness and stakeholder dependence reflect an operational reliability concern secondary to the JD's delivery focus, without regulatory or compliance framing, consistent with moderate loss aversion.
